description:
You are an on–call operations engineer.  
An application has started throwing errors and you have been asked to collect the faulty log files in a separate place and write a brief summary for the incident ticket.

The environment you will find when you open the terminal:

1. Directory /home/user/app/logs already exists.
2. Inside that directory there are exactly three log files:  
   • app.log  
   • db.log  
   • access.log  
3. Only app.log and db.log contain the string “ERROR” (uppercase, without quotes).  
   access.log contains no “ERROR” lines at all.

Your job

A. Create a new directory called /home/user/incidents/2023-07-30  
   • The intermediate directory /home/user/incidents **does not yet exist**, so make sure the full path is created.  

B. Move every “.log” file from /home/user/app/logs that contains at least one line with the word “ERROR” into the newly-created directory.  
   • After the move, /home/user/incidents/2023-07-30 must hold those files and **only** those files.  
   • /home/user/app/logs should still exist but should now be missing the moved files.

C. Inside /home/user/incidents/2023-07-30 create a text file named summary.txt with the following exact two-line format (no extra spaces or blank lines):

MovedFiles: <number_of_files_moved>
TotalErrorLines: <total_count_of_lines_containing_ERROR>

For this specific environment the values must match the actual files that were moved and the real count of lines containing the literal string “ERROR”.  
Be careful: the automated test will parse summary.txt and match both the key names and the integer values.

Verification notes that the judges will use (and that you must follow):

1. /home/user/incidents/2023-07-30 must exist and be a directory.  
2. /home/user/incidents/2023-07-30 must contain exactly:  
   • app.log  
   • db.log  
   • summary.txt  
   (No other files.)  
3. /home/user/app/logs must still exist and must now contain exactly:  
   • access.log  
4. summary.txt must be plain text encoded in UTF-8 and contain exactly two newline-separated lines in the precise format shown above.  
   Example shape (numbers will differ if your counts are wrong):  
   MovedFiles: 2  
   TotalErrorLines: 5  

You should be able to accomplish the whole task with roughly 2–3 reasonably composed shell commands. Use any standard GNU/Linux utilities available in a default container.  
Remember: do not include explanations or commentary in summary.txt—only the two lines shown in the required format.
truth:
Initial file state (before the agent starts):

/home/user/app/logs/app.log
 ├─ Line 1: 2023-07-30 04:01:22 INFO Application started
 ├─ Line 2: 2023-07-30 04:02:10 ERROR NullPointerException in ModuleX
 ├─ Line 3: 2023-07-30 04:03:55 WARN  Disk space low
 ├─ Line 4: 2023-07-30 04:04:01 ERROR Failed to load configuration
 └─ Line 5: 2023-07-30 04:05:30 ERROR OutOfMemoryError in Worker-12

/home/user/app/logs/db.log
 ├─ Line 1: 2023-07-30 04:02:11 INFO  Connection pool initialized
 ├─ Line 2: 2023-07-30 04:02:15 ERROR Lost connection to replica-2
 └─ Line 3: 2023-07-30 04:02:17 ERROR Automatic failover engaged

/home/user/app/logs/access.log
 ├─ Line 1: 127.0.0.1 - - [30/Jul/2023:04:00:01 +0000] "GET /healthz HTTP/1.1" 200 12
 └─ Line 2: 127.0.0.1 - - [30/Jul/2023:04:00:11 +0000] "GET /metrics HTTP/1.1" 200 231

Summary for verification:

• Number of files that contain “ERROR” = 2 (app.log, db.log)  
• Total “ERROR” lines across those files = 5 (3 in app.log + 2 in db.log)

Expected final files after the agent completes:

/home/user/incidents/2023-07-30/app.log            (exact same 5-line content as original)
/home/user/incidents/2023-07-30/db.log             (exact same 3-line content as original)
/home/user/incidents/2023-07-30/summary.txt        (contains: "MovedFiles: 2\nTotalErrorLines: 5\n")

Remaining file in original location:

/home/user/app/logs/access.log  (unchanged, still 2 lines)

No other files or directories should be created.
